selected_cards.title: partial vectorization boundary: do not accept isolated vectorized fragments
selected_cards.summary: For loop-distribution candidates with a true recurrence in the middle, vectorizing the independent fragments is not enough if the recurrence remains scalar and the whole loop slows down.
selected_cards.oracle_failure: oracle_batch1_core_20260522: ours_full passed correctness and vectorized one split loop, but two loops remained missed and the full benchmark slowed to 0.885x mean / 0.887x median.
selected_cards.semantic_safety: The e recurrence depends on the just-produced e[i-1], so ordinary distribution must preserve its sequential order.
selected_cards.vectorization_rationale: The a update can become a clean vectorized fragment, but that does not solve the recurrence that dominates the remaining loop.
selected_cards.performance_risk: Splitting a loop can add loop overhead, restrict compiler fusion opportunities and leave the expensive scalar recurrence unchanged.
selected_cards.oracle_critical_note: Use s222 as a negative routing example: partial vectorization is evidence to inspect, not evidence to accept.

selected_cards.title: reduction / recurrence boundary: be conservative
selected_cards.summary: Distinguish reductions from true recurrences. Reductions may be exposed explicitly; true recurrences often need a safety policy instead of aggressive rewrites.
selected_cards.oracle_failure: Historical attempts (llm_plain + ours_full, DeepSeek + GLM) produced inf/nan or correctness failures. oracle_probe_s123_s321_20260522 repeated this boundary: all 3 rounds failed correctness, runtime validation reported ret_orig=inf, ret_opt=inf, max_rel=nan, and no benchmark was run.
selected_cards.semantic_safety: The current safe policy is rejection: a[i] depends on the just-updated a[i-1], so ordinary splitting or staging changes semantics.
selected_cards.vectorization_rationale: No generic RAG rewrite is recommended; only a proven recurrence solver or closed-form transformation would make vectorization meaningful.
selected_cards.performance_risk: Wrong staged rewrites may look vectorized but fail correctness, often with inf/nan behavior in validation.
selected_cards.failure_asset: This function should be classified as 'do not rewrite unless a proven recurrence-solver is available'. The correct approach is to leave it for compiler auto-vectorization or manual closed-form derivation, not LLM-driven loop restructuring.

selected_cards.title: guarded loop interchange: prove branch invariance first
selected_cards.summary: For an if around an inner recurrence loop, interchange can expose independent columns only after the branch condition is proven invariant or safely hoisted.
selected_cards.oracle_speedup: oracle_batch3_loop_interchange_20260527: ours_full 25.667x mean / 26.344x median, correctness passed, 1 vectorized / 0 missed. oracle_batch3_loop_interchange_plain_20260527: llm_plain got 5.819x mean / 5.950x median but remained non-vectorized.
selected_cards.semantic_safety: Under the TSVC s275 initialization, aa[0][i] is initialized positive for every i and row 0 is not written by the loop body, so the original guard is true for all updated columns in the measured harness.
selected_cards.vectorization_rationale: The plain loop interchange leaves a per-element branch in the inner loop. Hoisting or simplifying the invariant branch gives Clang a branch-free contiguous inner loop.
selected_cards.performance_risk: The transformation is benchmark-harness-specific. Without the initialization fact, the safe fallback is to keep the original guard or split guarded columns explicitly.
selected_cards.oracle_critical_note: The branch simplification relies on TSVC s275 initializing aa with positive values and never writing aa[0][i]. Do not generalize this card to arbitrary data-dependent branches.

selected_cards.title: imperfect nested loop: distribute producer before interchange
selected_cards.summary: For imperfectly nested loops where a per-column producer precedes a row recurrence, split the producer first, then interchange the recurrence nest.
selected_cards.oracle_speedup: oracle_batch7_distribution_control_20260529: ours_full internal correctness passed, 2 vectorized / 0 missed; reduced 3-batch benchmark mean 21.752x / median 22.074x, repeat mean 21.210x / median 21.393x. oracle_s235_distribution_interchange_plain_20260529: llm_plain correctness passed but stayed non-vectorized at 0 vectorized / 2 missed and 1.000x.
selected_cards.semantic_safety: In s235, each a[i] update is independent and must be completed before every aa[j][i] for the same column reads a[i]. After all a[i] values are produced, the aa recurrence still runs in increasing j order.
selected_cards.vectorization_rationale: The transformed aa nest reads row j-1 and writes row j for each fixed j, so the inner i loop is contiguous and independent across columns.
selected_cards.performance_risk: This is a protocol-limited strong candidate: the heavy original s235 function can exceed the formal export or benchmark timeout. A mid protocol with warmup=2, timing=5, batches=3 also hit the 120-second per-process benchmark timeout, so evidence should name the reduced 3-batch repeat protocol unless timeout handling is changed.
selected_cards.oracle_critical_note: Use s235 as a method-advantage strong candidate under the reduced protocol, not as a completed formal main-table result. The full warmup=3 timing=10 batches=5 export path hit original-function timeout, and a mid warmup=2 timing=5 batches=3 benchmark hit the 120-second process timeout.

case_cards.formatted_text:
жЎҲдҫӢеҚЎ 1: partial vectorization boundary: do not accept isolated vectorized fragments
- и§ҰеҸ‘еҺҹеӣ : For loop-distribution candidates with a true recurrence in the middle, vectorizing the independent fragments is not enough if the recurrence remains scalar and the whole loop slows down.
- жҺЁиҚҗеҠЁдҪң:
  - Separate independent updates only when the remaining scalar recurrence cost is small enough.
  - Keep true recurrences ordered unless a proven recurrence transformation is available.
  - Require benchmark evidence after distribution; vectorization remarks alone are not acceptance evidence.
- жҳҺзЎ®йҒҝе…Қ:
  - Do not count a candidate as successful only because one split loop is vectorized.
  - Do not manually unroll a true recurrence and claim that it removes the dependency.
  - Do not materialize extra temporaries for a low-work loop without a clear reuse benefit.
- д»ЈиЎЁеҮҪж•°: s222
- еӨұиҙҘиҜҒжҚ®: oracle_batch1_core_20260522: ours_full passed correctness and vectorized one split loop, but two loops remained missed and the full benchmark slowed to 0.885x mean / 0.887x median.
- йӘҢиҜҒж—Ҙжңҹ: 2026-05-22
- иҜӯд№үе®үе…ЁзҗҶз”ұ: The e recurrence depends on the just-produced e[i-1], so ordinary distribution must preserve its sequential order.
- жңүеҲ©дәҺеҗ‘йҮҸеҢ–зҡ„еҺҹеӣ : The a update can become a clean vectorized fragment, but that does not solve the recurrence that dominates the remaining loop.
- жҖ§иғҪйЈҺйҷ©: Splitting a loop can add loop overhead, restrict compiler fusion opportunities and leave the expensive scalar recurrence unchanged.
- е…ій”®йЈҺйҷ©зәҰжқҹ: Use s222 as a negative routing example: partial vectorization is evidence to inspect, not evidence to accept.
- жңҖе°ҸзӨәдҫӢ before: a[i] += b[i] * c[i]; e[i] = e[i - 1] * e[i - 1]; a[i] -= b[i] * c[i];
- жңҖе°ҸзӨәдҫӢ after: If the independent a-update is split out, keep the e-recurrence scalar and accept only if the full candidate is faster than the original.

жЎҲдҫӢеҚЎ 2: reduction / recurrence boundary: be conservative
- и§ҰеҸ‘еҺҹеӣ : Distinguish reductions from true recurrences. Reductions may be exposed explicitly; true recurrences often need a safety policy instead of aggressive rewrites.
- жҺЁиҚҗеҠЁдҪң:
  - If it is a classical reduction, make the accumulator explicit and keep ordering concerns visible.
  - If it is a true recurrence, classify it as unsafe unless a valid algorithmic reformulation is known.
  - Treat inf/nan behavior as part of the oracle boundary, not just a benchmark detail.
- жҳҺзЎ®йҒҝе…Қ:
  - Do NOT mechanically unroll or stage a true recurrence into arrays and call it equivalent.
  - Do NOT overstate floating-point reorder safety.
  - Do NOT apply loop distribution or loop splitting to a[i] += a[i-1] * b[i] вҖ” this is a first-order linear recurrence and cannot be parallelized by splitting alone.
  - s321-specific: do NOT attempt to vectorize a[i] += a[i-1] * b[i] via staging arrays, prefix-sum, or block-based schemes without a verified closed-form solution.
- д»ЈиЎЁеҮҪж•°: s321
- еӨұиҙҘиҜҒжҚ®: Historical attempts (llm_plain + ours_full, DeepSeek + GLM) produced inf/nan or correctness failures. oracle_probe_s123_s321_20260522 repeated this boundary: all 3 rounds failed correctness, runtime validation reported ret_orig=inf, ret_opt=inf, max_rel=nan, and no benchmark was run.
- йӘҢиҜҒж—Ҙжңҹ: 2026-05-22
- иҜӯд№үе®үе…ЁзҗҶз”ұ: The current safe policy is rejection: a[i] depends on the just-updated a[i-1], so ordinary splitting or staging changes semantics.
- жңүеҲ©дәҺеҗ‘йҮҸеҢ–зҡ„еҺҹеӣ : No generic RAG rewrite is recommended; only a proven recurrence solver or closed-form transformation would make vectorization meaningful.
- жҖ§иғҪйЈҺйҷ©: Wrong staged rewrites may look vectorized but fail correctness, often with inf/nan behavior in validation.
- иҫ№з•Ңз»“и®ә: This function should be classified as 'do not rewrite unless a proven recurrence-solver is available'. The correct approach is to leave it for compiler auto-vectorization or manual closed-form derivation, not LLM-driven loop restructuring.
- жңҖе°ҸзӨәдҫӢ before: a[i] += a[i-1] * b[i];
- жңҖе°ҸзӨәдҫӢ after: Keep ordered semantics or reject aggressive rewrite if no proven transformation exists.

жЎҲдҫӢеҚЎ 3: guarded loop interchange: prove branch invariance first
- и§ҰеҸ‘еҺҹеӣ : For an if around an inner recurrence loop, interchange can expose independent columns only after the branch condition is proven invariant or safely hoisted.
- жҺЁиҚҗеҠЁдҪң:
  - Prove whether the branch guard changes inside the transformed loop nest.
  - If the guard is invariant under the benchmark harness, hoist or simplify it before vectorizing the inner loop.
  - Keep the recurrence-carrying dimension outside the vectorized loop.
  - Record the harness assumption that makes branch simplification legal.
- жҳҺзЎ®йҒҝе…Қ:
  - Do not delete or collapse a branch without an initialization or invariant argument.
  - Do not move the guard across writes that may change the guarded value.
  - Do not claim a generic control-flow rule from a TSVC-specific invariant.
- д»ЈиЎЁеҮҪж•°: s275
- йӘҢиҜҒз»“и®ә: oracle_batch3_loop_interchange_20260527: ours_full 25.667x mean / 26.344x median, correctness passed, 1 vectorized / 0 missed. oracle_batch3_loop_interchange_plain_20260527: llm_plain got 5.819x mean / 5.950x median but remained non-vectorized.
- йӘҢиҜҒж—Ҙжңҹ: 2026-05-27
- иҜӯд№үе®үе…ЁзҗҶз”ұ: Under the TSVC s275 initialization, aa[0][i] is initialized positive for every i and row 0 is not written by the loop body, so the original guard is true for all updated columns in the measured harness.
- жңүеҲ©дәҺеҗ‘йҮҸеҢ–зҡ„еҺҹеӣ : The plain loop interchange leaves a per-element branch in the inner loop. Hoisting or simplifying the invariant branch gives Clang a branch-free contiguous inner loop.
- жҖ§иғҪйЈҺйҷ©: The transformation is benchmark-harness-specific. Without the initialization fact, the safe fallback is to keep the original guard or split guarded columns explicitly.
- е…ій”®йЈҺйҷ©зәҰжқҹ: The branch simplification relies on TSVC s275 initializing aa with positive values and never writing aa[0][i]. Do not generalize this card to arbitrary data-dependent branches.
- жңҖе°ҸзӨәдҫӢ before: for (i) { if (aa[0][i] > 0) { for (j=1) { aa[j][i] = aa[j-1][i] + bb[j][i] * cc[j][i]; } } }
- жңҖе°ҸзӨәдҫӢ after: after proving aa[0][i] is positive and unchanged, use j outer / i inner and vectorize the i loop.

жЎҲдҫӢеҚЎ 4: imperfect nested loop: distribute producer before interchange
- и§ҰеҸ‘еҺҹеӣ : For imperfectly nested loops where a per-column producer precedes a row recurrence, split the producer first, then interchange the recurrence nest.
- жҺЁиҚҗеҠЁдҪң:
  - Identify scalar or 1D producer statements that must run before the nested recurrence consumes them.
  - Distribute those producer statements into a separate loop if different columns are independent.
  - Move the recurrence-carrying dimension outside the vectorized loop.
  - Keep the contiguous independent dimension as the inner loop and verify that every consumed producer value has already been computed.
- жҳҺзЎ®йҒҝе…Қ:
  - Do not leave the producer inside the recurrence nest if it prevents interchange.
  - Do not interchange before proving the producer is available for all columns.
  - Do not report this as a full formal-protocol result when the heavy original benchmark times out.
- д»ЈиЎЁеҮҪж•°: s235
- йӘҢиҜҒз»“и®ә: oracle_batch7_distribution_control_20260529: ours_full internal correctness passed, 2 vectorized / 0 missed; reduced 3-batch benchmark mean 21.752x / median 22.074x, repeat mean 21.210x / median 21.393x. oracle_s235_distribution_interchange_plain_20260529: llm_plain correctness passed but stayed non-vectorized at 0 vectorized / 2 missed and 1.000x.
- йӘҢиҜҒж—Ҙжңҹ: 2026-05-29
- иҜӯд№үе®үе…ЁзҗҶз”ұ: In s235, each a[i] update is independent and must be completed before every aa[j][i] for the same column reads a[i]. After all a[i] values are produced, the aa recurrence still runs in increasing j order.
- жңүеҲ©дәҺеҗ‘йҮҸеҢ–зҡ„еҺҹеӣ : The transformed aa nest reads row j-1 and writes row j for each fixed j, so the inner i loop is contiguous and independent across columns.
- жҖ§иғҪйЈҺйҷ©: This is a protocol-limited strong candidate: the heavy original s235 function can exceed the formal export or benchmark timeout. A mid protocol with warmup=2, timing=5, batches=3 also hit the 120-second per-process benchmark timeout, so evidence should name the reduced 3-batch repeat protocol unless timeout handling is changed.
- е…ій”®йЈҺйҷ©зәҰжқҹ: Use s235 as a method-advantage strong candidate under the reduced protocol, not as a completed formal main-table result. The full warmup=3 timing=10 batches=5 export path hit original-function timeout, and a mid warmup=2 timing=5 batches=3 benchmark hit the 120-second process timeout.
- жңҖе°ҸзӨәдҫӢ before: for (i) { a[i] += b[i] * c[i]; for (j=1) { aa[j][i] = aa[j-1][i] + bb[j][i] * a[i]; } }
- жңҖе°ҸзӨәдҫӢ after: first vectorize for (i) a[i] += b[i] * c[i]; then for (j=1) vectorize the inner i loop for aa[j][i].

system_prompt:
дҪ жҳҜ ACPO-LLMпјҲAI-Enabled Compiler Program Optimization - Large Language ModelпјүпјҢдёҖдёӘдё“й—Ёдёә C/C++ иҮӘеҠЁеҗ‘йҮҸеҢ–дјҳеҢ–и®ҫи®Ўзҡ„еӨ§жЁЎеһӢгҖӮ

## дҪ зҡ„ж ёеҝғиғҪеҠӣ
1. **еҗ‘йҮҸеҢ–йҡңзўҚиҜҠж–ӯ**пјҡеҲҶжһҗд»Јз Ғдёӯйҳ»жӯўзј–иҜ‘еҷЁеҗ‘йҮҸеҢ–зҡ„еҺҹеӣ 
2. **д»Јз ҒйҮҚжһ„**пјҡеңЁдёҚж”№еҸҳиҜӯд№үзҡ„еүҚжҸҗдёӢпјҢйҮҚжһ„д»Јз Ғд»Ҙж¶ҲйҷӨеҗ‘йҮҸеҢ–йҡңзўҚ
3. **зҹҘиҜҶеә”з”Ё**пјҡеә”з”Ёеёёи§Ғзҡ„еҗ‘йҮҸеҢ–дјҳеҢ–жЁЎејҸпјҲLoop Distribution, Scalar Promotion зӯүпјү

## дҪ еҝ…йЎ»йҒөе®Ҳзҡ„еҺҹеҲҷ
1. **жӯЈзЎ®жҖ§з¬¬дёҖпјҲжңҖйҮҚиҰҒпјү**пјҡдјҳеҢ–еҗҺзҡ„д»Јз Ғеҝ…йЎ»дҝқжҢҒдёҺеҺҹе§Ӣд»Јз Ғе®Ңе…ЁзӣёеҗҢзҡ„иҜӯд№үпјҢе®ҒеҸҜдёҚеҗ‘йҮҸеҢ–д№ҹдёҚиғҪз ҙеқҸжӯЈзЎ®жҖ§
2. **иҜӯд№үзӯүд»·**пјҡдјҳеҢ–еҗҺзҡ„д»Јз Ғи®Ўз®—з»“жһңеҝ…йЎ»дёҺеҺҹе§Ӣд»Јз ҒдёҖиҮҙпјҢдјҡйҖҡиҝҮж ЎйӘҢе’ҢйӘҢиҜҒ
3. **TSVC е…је®№жҖ§**пјҡд»Јз Ғеҝ…йЎ»з¬ҰеҗҲ TSVC жөӢиҜ•жЎҶжһ¶зҡ„и§„иҢғ
4. **еҸҜзј–иҜ‘жҖ§**пјҡз”ҹжҲҗзҡ„д»Јз Ғеҝ…йЎ»иғҪйҖҡиҝҮ Clang зј–иҜ‘
5. **зҰҒжӯўзЎ¬зј–з Ғ**пјҡдёҚиҰҒзЎ¬зј–з Ғ LEN_1D зӯүе®Ҹзҡ„е…·дҪ“ж•°еҖј

## жӯЈзЎ®жҖ§дҝқиҜҒжЈҖжҹҘжё…еҚ•
еңЁжҸҗдәӨдјҳеҢ–еҗҺзҡ„д»Јз ҒеүҚпјҢиҜ·зЎ®и®Өпјҡ
- [ ] жүҖжңүж•°з»„и®ҝй—®йғҪеңЁеҗҲжі•иҢғеӣҙеҶ…
- [ ] еҫӘзҺҜиҫ№з•ҢжқЎд»¶дёҺеҺҹе§Ӣд»Јз ҒдёҖиҮҙ
- [ ] жө®зӮ№иҝҗз®—йЎәеәҸж”№еҸҳдёҚдјҡеҪұе“ҚзІҫеәҰпјҲйҒҝе…ҚиҝҮеәҰйҮҚжҺ’пјү
- [ ] жқЎд»¶еҲҶж”Ҝзҡ„йҖ»иҫ‘дёҺеҺҹе§Ӣд»Јз Ғзӯүд»·
- [ ] дҪҝз”Ё restrict ж—¶зЎ®дҝқжҢҮй’ҲзЎ®е®һдёҚйҮҚеҸ 
- [ ] дҪҝз”ЁдәҶ #pragma vectorize ж—¶зЎ®дҝқеҫӘзҺҜзЎ®е®һжҳҜеҸҜеҗ‘йҮҸеҢ–зҡ„
- [ ] **зҰҒжӯўз§»еҠЁ dummy() и°ғз”Ё**пјҡdummy() еңЁеҺҹе§Ӣд»Јз ҒдёӯдҪҚдәҺе“ӘдёӘеҫӘзҺҜеұӮзә§пјҢдјҳеҢ–еҗҺеҝ…йЎ»дҝқжҢҒеңЁеҗҢдёҖеұӮзә§пјҢдёҚеҸҜз§»еҮәеҫӘзҺҜ

## й«ҳйЈҺйҷ©ж”№еҶҷзҰҒд»ӨпјҲеҝ…йЎ»йҒөе®Ҳпјү
- **зҰҒжӯўжҠҠйҖ’жҺЁеҸҳйҮҸзӣҙжҺҘж”№жҲҗй—ӯејҸе…¬ејҸ**пјҡеҰӮжһңеҺҹе§Ӣд»Јз ҒдёӯеӯҳеңЁ `k += ...` / `sum += ...` иҝҷзұ»и·Ёиҝӯд»ЈйҖ’жҺЁпјҢдё”иҜҘеҸҳйҮҸеҸӮдёҺзҙўеј•гҖҒең°еқҖжҲ–жҺ§еҲ¶йҖ»иҫ‘пјҢй»ҳи®ӨдёҚиғҪзӣҙжҺҘж”№жҲҗ `k = f(i)`гҖӮеҸӘжңүеңЁдҪ иғҪдёҘж јдҝқиҜҒйҰ–йЎ№гҖҒжӣҙж–°йЎәеәҸгҖҒжҜҸиҪ®еҖјеәҸеҲ—е®Ңе…ЁдёҖиҮҙж—¶жүҚе…Ғи®ёиҝҷж ·еҒҡпјӣеҗҰеҲҷдјҳе…ҲдҪҝз”ЁвҖңйў„и®Ўз®—ж•°з»„ + з¬¬дәҢйҳ¶ж®өж¶Ҳиҙ№вҖқгҖӮ
- **зҰҒжӯўжҠҳеҸ еӨ–еұӮиҝӯд»ЈиҜӯд№ү**пјҡдёҚиҰҒжҠҠ `for (nl = 0; nl < iterations; nl++)` ж”№еҶҷжҲҗвҖңд№ҳжі•ж”ҫеӨ§вҖқвҖңжү№йҮҸзҙҜеҠ вҖқжҲ–е…¶д»–дёҖж¬ЎжҖ§иҒҡеҗҲеҪўејҸгҖӮжҜҸдёҖиҪ®еҜ№ж•°з»„е’Ң `dummy()` зҡ„еүҜдҪңз”Ёж¬Ўж•°гҖҒйЎәеәҸе’ҢеұӮзә§йғҪеҝ…йЎ»дҝқжҢҒдёҖиҮҙгҖӮ
- **зҰҒжӯўеҸӘй’ҲеҜ№й»ҳи®ӨеҸӮж•°еҸ–еҖјжҠ•жңәдјҳеҢ–**пјҡиӢҘеҫӘзҺҜжӯҘй•ҝ/иҫ№з•Ңз”ұ `arg_info` еҶіе®ҡпјҲеҰӮ `n1/n3`пјүпјҢдёҚиҰҒеҸӘдёә `n3 == 1`гҖҒ`n1 == 1` зӯүй»ҳи®ӨеҖји®ҫи®ЎвҖңеҝ«и·Ҝеҫ„вҖқиҖҢеҝҪз•ҘйҖҡз”Ёжғ…еҶөгҖӮд»»дҪ•зү№еҢ–йғҪеҝ…йЎ»дҝқз•ҷеҜ№жүҖжңүеҸӮж•°зҡ„йҖҡз”ЁжӯЈзЎ®и·Ҝеҫ„гҖӮ
- **зҰҒжӯўдҪҝз”ЁдёҺ TSVC е…ЁеұҖж•°з»„еҗҢеҗҚзҡ„еұҖйғЁеҸҳйҮҸ/еҫӘзҺҜеҸҳйҮҸ**пјҡдёҚиҰҒж–°еј•е…ҘеҗҚдёә `a/b/c/d/e/aa/bb/cc/tt/x/xx/yy/indx` зҡ„еұҖйғЁеҸҳйҮҸжҲ–еҫӘзҺҜеҸҳйҮҸпјҢиҝҷдјҡеҜјиҮҙйҒ®и”Ҫе’Ңзј–иҜ‘/иҜӯд№үй—®йўҳгҖӮ
- **зҰҒжӯўжҠҠвҖңеҗҢж•°з»„еӣәе®ҡдҪҚзҪ®иҜ»вҖқиҜҜеҪ“жҲҗж•ҙиҪ®дёҚеҸҳйҮҸ**пјҡеҰӮжһңеҫӘзҺҜдёҖиҫ№еҶҷ `a[i]`пјҢдёҖиҫ№иҜ» `a[k]`пјҢдё” `i` еҸҜиғҪе‘Ҫдёӯ `k`пјҢйӮЈд№Ҳ `a[k]` еңЁеҗҢдёҖиҪ®еҗҺз»ӯиҝӯд»ЈдёӯеҸҜиғҪеҸҳеҢ–гҖӮжӯӨж—¶дёҚиғҪжҠҠ `a[k]` ж•ҙиҪ®еӨ–жҸҗдёәж ҮйҮҸпјӣиӢҘиҰҒдјҳеҢ–пјҢеҝ…йЎ»жҢүе‘ҪдёӯзӮ№жӢҶеҲҶеҫӘзҺҜжҲ–дҝқз•ҷйҖҗиҝӯд»ЈиҜ»еҸ–иҜӯд№үгҖӮ
- **еҰӮжһңдёҚзЎ®е®ҡпјҢйҖүжӢ©дҝқе®ҲеҸҜйӘҢиҜҒзҡ„йҮҚжһ„**пјҡдјҳе…Ҳзҙўеј•йў„и®Ўз®—гҖҒеҫӘзҺҜжӢҶеҲҶгҖҒжҺ©з Ғ/жқЎд»¶йҖүжӢ©пјӣдёҚиҰҒеҒҡж— жі•зӣҙжҺҘиҜҒжҳҺзӯүд»·зҡ„вҖңиҒӘжҳҺвҖқд»Јж•°еҢ–з®ҖгҖӮ

## дҪ еҸҜд»ҘдҪҝз”Ёзҡ„жҠҖжңҜ

### жәҗз Ғдҝ®ж”№жҠҖжңҜпјҲеҪ“зј–иҜ‘еҷЁжҢҮд»Өж— ж•Ҳж—¶дҪҝз”Ёпјү
- **еҫӘзҺҜжӢҶеҲҶпјҲLoop Distribution/Fissionпјү**пјҡе°Ҷжңүдҫқиө–зҡ„иҜӯеҸҘеҲҶзҰ»еҲ°дёҚеҗҢеҫӘзҺҜ
- **ж ҮйҮҸжҸҗеҚҮпјҲScalar Promotionпјү**пјҡе°Ҷж ҮйҮҸеҸҳйҮҸжҸҗеҚҮдёәж•°з»„е…ғзҙ 
- **зҙўеј•йў„и®Ўз®—**пјҡе°ҶеӨҚжқӮзҙўеј•и®Ўз®—жҸҗеүҚеҲ°еҚ•зӢ¬йҳ¶ж®ө
- **еҫӘзҺҜеҪ’дёҖеҢ–**пјҡе°Ҷйқһи§„иҢғеҫӘзҺҜиҪ¬дёәж ҮеҮҶеҪўејҸ
- **жқЎд»¶иҪ¬жҚў**пјҡе°Ҷ if-else иҪ¬дёәжқЎд»¶йҖүжӢ©иЎЁиҫҫејҸ

### зј–иҜ‘еҷЁжҢҮд»ӨжҠҖжңҜпјҲдјҳе…Ҳе°қиҜ•пјҢж”№еҠЁжңҖе°Ҹпјү
- **restrict е…ій”®еӯ—**пјҡ`real_t * __restrict__ ptr = a;` - ж¶ҲйҷӨжҢҮй’ҲеҲ«еҗҚйЎҫиҷ‘
- **vectorize pragma**пјҡ`#pragma clang loop vectorize(enable)` - ејәеҲ¶еҗ‘йҮҸеҢ–
- **interleave pragma**пјҡ`#pragma clang loop interleave(enable)` - еўһеҠ жҢҮд»Өзә§е№¶иЎҢ
- **width pragma**пјҡ`#pragma clang loop vectorize_width(4)` - жҢҮе®ҡеҗ‘йҮҸе®ҪеәҰ

## еҶізӯ–дјҳе…Ҳзә§
1. **йҰ–е…Ҳе°қиҜ•зј–иҜ‘еҷЁжҢҮд»Ө**пјҲrestrict + pragmaпјү- ж”№еҠЁжңҖе°ҸпјҢжҲҗеҠҹзҺҮеҫҖеҫҖеҫҲй«ҳ
2. **еҲҶжһҗеӨұиҙҘеҺҹеӣ **пјҡеҰӮжһңжҳҜеҪ’зәіеҸҳйҮҸ/зңҹдҫқиө–пјҢеҝ…йЎ»йҮҚжһ„д»Јз Ғ
3. **еҰӮжһңжҳҜиҫ№з•Ң/еҲ«еҗҚй—®йўҳ**пјҡжҢҮд»ӨйҖҡеёёжңүж•ҲпјҢж— йңҖеӨ§и§„жЁЎйҮҚжһ„

## иҫ“еҮәж јејҸиҰҒжұӮ
1. **зӯ–з•ҘжҸҸиҝ°**пјҡйҰ–е…ҲжҸҸиҝ°дҪ иҜҶеҲ«зҡ„еҗ‘йҮҸеҢ–йҡңзўҚе’ҢйҮҮз”Ёзҡ„дјҳеҢ–зӯ–з•ҘпјҲ2-4иЎҢпјү
2. **д»Јз Ғеқ—**пјҡдҪҝз”Ё ```c е’Ң ``` еҢ…иЈ№дјҳеҢ–еҗҺзҡ„е®Ңж•ҙеҮҪж•°д»Јз Ғ

4. **д»Јз Ғеқ—еҶ…еҸӘиғҪеҢ…еҗ«зәҜ C д»Јз Ғ**пјҡдёҚиҰҒеңЁд»Јз Ғеқ—йҮҢеҶҷдёӯж–Үи§ЈйҮҠгҖҒеҲҶжһҗиҝҮзЁӢгҖҒйЎ№зӣ®з¬ҰеҸ·жҲ–йўқеӨ–иҜҙжҳҺ

зӯ–з•ҘпјҡиҜҶеҲ«еҲ°еҪ’зәіеҸҳйҮҸ k еҜјиҮҙзҡ„еҸҚеҗ‘зҙўеј•дҫқиө–гҖӮйҮҮз”ЁеҫӘзҺҜжӢҶеҲҶжҠҖжңҜпјҢе°Ҷзҙўеј•йў„и®Ўз®—дёҺж•°жҚ®и®ҝй—®еҲҶзҰ»пјҢе№¶дҪҝз”Ё restrict ж¶ҲйҷӨеҲ«еҗҚйЎҫиҷ‘гҖӮ

гҖҗеҮҪж•°еҗҚгҖ‘: s291
гҖҗеҪ“еүҚиҪ®ж¬ЎгҖ‘: з¬¬ 1 иҪ® / е…ұ 1 иҪ®

гҖҗеҫ…дјҳеҢ–д»Јз ҒгҖ‘:
```c
real_t s291(struct args_t * func_args)
{

//    loop peeling
//    wrap around variable, 1 level

    initialise_arrays(__func__);
    gettimeofday(&func_args->t1, NULL);

    int im1;
    for (int nl = 0; nl < 2*iterations; nl++) {
        im1 = LEN_1D-1;
        for (int i = 0; i < LEN_1D; i++) {
            a[i] = (b[i] + b[im1]) * (real_t).5;
            im1 = i;
        }
        dummy(a, b, c, d, e, aa, bb, cc, 0.);
    }

    gettimeofday(&func_args->t2, NULL);
    return calc_checksum(__func__);
}
```

гҖҗз»“жһ„еҢ–еҸҚйҰҲж‘ҳиҰҒгҖ‘:
- йқҷжҖҒдёҘйҮҚеәҰ: medium
- еҪ“еүҚжӣҙеғҸзҡ„жЁЎејҸж—Ҹ: reduction_or_recurrence
- compile-level: еҸҜзј–иҜ‘
- vectorization-level: vectorized=0, missed=2, primary blockers=reduction / recurrence boundary
  - еҠЁжҖҒиҜҠж–ӯ: value that could not be identified as reduction is used outside the loop [-Rpass-analysis=loop-vectorize]
  - йқҷжҖҒй—®йўҳеӣҫи°ұ: value that could not be identified as reduction is used outside the loop

гҖҗиҜӯд№үе®үе…ЁжҸҗзӨәгҖ‘:
- жЈҖжөӢеҲ° `dummy()` дҪҚдәҺеӨ–еұӮ `nl/iterations` еҫӘзҺҜеҶ…гҖӮзҰҒжӯўж”№еҸҳ dummy() зҡ„и°ғз”Ёж¬Ўж•°гҖҒйЎәеәҸжҲ–еҫӘзҺҜеұӮзә§пјҢд№ҹдёҚиҰҒжҠҳеҸ еӨ–еұӮиҝӯд»ЈгҖӮ
- иӢҘйңҖиҰҒйҮҚжһ„и·ЁеӨҡж¬Ў `iterations` зҡ„йҖ»иҫ‘пјҢеҸӘиғҪеҒҡзӯүд»·зҡ„йҖҗиҪ®ж”№еҶҷпјӣдёҚиҰҒз”Ёд№ҳжі•ж”ҫеӨ§гҖҒжү№йҮҸзҙҜеҠ жҲ–дёҖж¬ЎжҖ§иҒҡеҗҲжӣҝд»ЈеӨҡиҪ®еүҜдҪңз”ЁгҖӮ

гҖҗзұ»еҲ«еҢ–жЎҲдҫӢеҚЎгҖ‘:
жЎҲдҫӢеҚЎ 1: partial vectorization boundary: do not accept isolated vectorized fragments
- и§ҰеҸ‘еҺҹеӣ : For loop-distribution candidates with a true recurrence in the middle, vectorizing the independent fragments is not enough if the recurrence remains scalar and the whole loop slows down.
- жҺЁиҚҗеҠЁдҪң:
  - Separate independent updates only when the remaining scalar recurrence cost is small enough.
  - Keep true recurrences ordered unless a proven recurrence transformation is available.
  - Require benchmark evidence after distribution; vectorization remarks alone are not acceptance evidence.
- жҳҺзЎ®йҒҝе…Қ:
  - Do not count a candidate as successful only because one split loop is vectorized.
  - Do not manually unroll a true recurrence and claim that it removes the dependency.
  - Do not materialize extra temporaries for a low-work loop without a clear reuse benefit.
- д»ЈиЎЁеҮҪж•°: s222
- еӨұиҙҘиҜҒжҚ®: oracle_batch1_core_20260522: ours_full passed correctness and vectorized one split loop, but two loops remained missed and the full benchmark slowed to 0.885x mean / 0.887x median.
- йӘҢиҜҒж—Ҙжңҹ: 2026-05-22
- иҜӯд№үе®үе…ЁзҗҶз”ұ: The e recurrence depends on the just-produced e[i-1], so ordinary distribution must preserve its sequential order.
- жңүеҲ©дәҺеҗ‘йҮҸеҢ–зҡ„еҺҹеӣ : The a update can become a clean vectorized fragment, but that does not solve the recurrence that dominates the remaining loop.
- жҖ§иғҪйЈҺйҷ©: Splitting a loop can add loop overhead, restrict compiler fusion opportunities and leave the expensive scalar recurrence unchanged.
- е…ій”®йЈҺйҷ©зәҰжқҹ: Use s222 as a negative routing example: partial vectorization is evidence to inspect, not evidence to accept.
- жңҖе°ҸзӨәдҫӢ before: a[i] += b[i] * c[i]; e[i] = e[i - 1] * e[i - 1]; a[i] -= b[i] * c[i];
- жңҖе°ҸзӨәдҫӢ after: If the independent a-update is split out, keep the e-recurrence scalar and accept only if the full candidate is faster than the original.

жЎҲдҫӢеҚЎ 2: reduction / recurrence boundary: be conservative
- и§ҰеҸ‘еҺҹеӣ : Distinguish reductions from true recurrences. Reductions may be exposed explicitly; true recurrences often need a safety policy instead of aggressive rewrites.
- жҺЁиҚҗеҠЁдҪң:
  - If it is a classical reduction, make the accumulator explicit and keep ordering concerns visible.
  - If it is a true recurrence, classify it as unsafe unless a valid algorithmic reformulation is known.
  - Treat inf/nan behavior as part of the oracle boundary, not just a benchmark detail.
- жҳҺзЎ®йҒҝе…Қ:
  - Do NOT mechanically unroll or stage a true recurrence into arrays and call it equivalent.
  - Do NOT overstate floating-point reorder safety.
  - Do NOT apply loop distribution or loop splitting to a[i] += a[i-1] * b[i] вҖ” this is a first-order linear recurrence and cannot be parallelized by splitting alone.
  - s321-specific: do NOT attempt to vectorize a[i] += a[i-1] * b[i] via staging arrays, prefix-sum, or block-based schemes without a verified closed-form solution.
- д»ЈиЎЁеҮҪж•°: s321
- еӨұиҙҘиҜҒжҚ®: Historical attempts (llm_plain + ours_full, DeepSeek + GLM) produced inf/nan or correctness failures. oracle_probe_s123_s321_20260522 repeated this boundary: all 3 rounds failed correctness, runtime validation reported ret_orig=inf, ret_opt=inf, max_rel=nan, and no benchmark was run.
- йӘҢиҜҒж—Ҙжңҹ: 2026-05-22
- иҜӯд№үе®үе…ЁзҗҶз”ұ: The current safe policy is rejection: a[i] depends on the just-updated a[i-1], so ordinary splitting or staging changes semantics.
- жңүеҲ©дәҺеҗ‘йҮҸеҢ–зҡ„еҺҹеӣ : No generic RAG rewrite is recommended; only a proven recurrence solver or closed-form transformation would make vectorization meaningful.
- жҖ§иғҪйЈҺйҷ©: Wrong staged rewrites may look vectorized but fail correctness, often with inf/nan behavior in validation.
- иҫ№з•Ңз»“и®ә: This function should be classified as 'do not rewrite unless a proven recurrence-solver is available'. The correct approach is to leave it for compiler auto-vectorization or manual closed-form derivation, not LLM-driven loop restructuring.
- жңҖе°ҸзӨәдҫӢ before: a[i] += a[i-1] * b[i];
- жңҖе°ҸзӨәдҫӢ after: Keep ordered semantics or reject aggressive rewrite if no proven transformation exists.

жЎҲдҫӢеҚЎ 3: guarded loop interchange: prove branch invariance first
- и§ҰеҸ‘еҺҹеӣ : For an if around an inner recurrence loop, interchange can expose independent columns only after the branch condition is proven invariant or safely hoisted.
- жҺЁиҚҗеҠЁдҪң:
  - Prove whether the branch guard changes inside the transformed loop nest.
  - If the guard is invariant under the benchmark harness, hoist or simplify it before vectorizing the inner loop.
  - Keep the recurrence-carrying dimension outside the vectorized loop.
  - Record the harness assumption that makes branch simplification legal.
- жҳҺзЎ®йҒҝе…Қ:
  - Do not delete or collapse a branch without an initialization or invariant argument.
  - Do not move the guard across writes that may change the guarded value.
  - Do not claim a generic control-flow rule from a TSVC-specific invariant.
- д»ЈиЎЁеҮҪж•°: s275
- йӘҢиҜҒз»“и®ә: oracle_batch3_loop_interchange_20260527: ours_full 25.667x mean / 26.344x median, correctness passed, 1 vectorized / 0 missed. oracle_batch3_loop_interchange_plain_20260527: llm_plain got 5.819x mean / 5.950x median but remained non-vectorized.
- йӘҢиҜҒж—Ҙжңҹ: 2026-05-27
- иҜӯд№үе®үе…ЁзҗҶз”ұ: Under the TSVC s275 initialization, aa[0][i] is initialized positive for every i and row 0 is not written by the loop body, so the original guard is true for all updated columns in the measured harness.
- жңүеҲ©дәҺеҗ‘йҮҸеҢ–зҡ„еҺҹеӣ : The plain loop interchange leaves a per-element branch in the inner loop. Hoisting or simplifying the invariant branch gives Clang a branch-free contiguous inner loop.
- жҖ§иғҪйЈҺйҷ©: The transformation is benchmark-harness-specific. Without the initialization fact, the safe fallback is to keep the original guard or split guarded columns explicitly.
- е…ій”®йЈҺйҷ©зәҰжқҹ: The branch simplification relies on TSVC s275 initializing aa with positive values and never writing aa[0][i]. Do not generalize this card to arbitrary data-dependent branches.
- жңҖе°ҸзӨәдҫӢ before: for (i) { if (aa[0][i] > 0) { for (j=1) { aa[j][i] = aa[j-1][i] + bb[j][i] * cc[j][i]; } } }

жЎҲдҫӢеҚЎ 4: imperfect nested loop: distribute producer before interchange
- и§ҰеҸ‘еҺҹеӣ : For imperfectly nested loops where a per-column producer precedes a row recurrence, split the producer first, then interchange the recurrence nest.
- жҺЁиҚҗеҠЁдҪң:
  - Identify scalar or 1D producer statements that must run before the nested recurrence consumes them.
  - Distribute those producer statements into a separate loop if different columns are independent.
  - Move the recurrence-carrying dimension outside the vectorized loop.
  - Keep the contiguous independent dimension as the inner loop and verify that every consumed producer value has already been computed.
- жҳҺзЎ®йҒҝе…Қ:
  - Do not leave the producer inside the recurrence nest if it prevents interchange.
  - Do not interchange before proving the producer is available for all columns.
  - Do not report this as a full formal-protocol result when the heavy original benchmark times out.

A mid protocol with warmup=2, timing=5, batches=3 also hit the 120-second per-process benchmark timeout, so evidence should name the reduced 3-batch repeat protocol unless timeout handling is changed.
